problem.title: Set Matrix Zeroes
problem.description: Given an m x n integer matrix matrix, if an element is 0, set its entire row and column to 0's.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Use First Row/Column as Markers
solutions.description: Instead of using extra space, repurpose the first row and first column of the matrix as boolean marker arrays. First record whether the first row/column themselves contain any zeros, then use the rest of the matrix to mark which rows and columns need zeroing. The key insight is that the first row/column can safely encode this state because they are processed last.
